name: SMC Global Power
full_name: San Miguel Global Power Holdings Corp

identification.ownership_details: SMC Group; Ramon Ang family controls ~38-39% effective economic interest via Top Frontier Investment Holdings (66% of SMC) and Privado Holdings (15.73% of SMC)
identification.listed_entity: Parent company SMC listed; SMCGP is unlisted subsidiary

credit_ratings.notes: Local rating only; deliberately unrated by international agencies

coal.notes: Operating coal capacity; 700 MW under construction (Masinloc 4-5)

hydro.notes: San Roque (345 MW) + Angat (218 MW, 60% owned)

battery_storage.notes: Largest BESS network in Philippines, completed April 2023

capacity.re_share_notes: Hydro only (563 MW of 6,080 MW). Zero solar/wind.

generation.notes: Coal dominates generation at 83% despite capacity mix

coal_plants.notes: SMC is the most aggressive coal developer in Philippines. 3,756 MW operating, 700 MW under construction. NO announced retirement dates for any plants.
coal_plants.retirement_methodology: No retirement dates announced - using 40-year technical life estimates

plants.name: Sual Power Station Units 1-2
plants.notes: BOT scheme expired October 2024; now fully owned. 26 years old.

plants.name: Masinloc Unit 1
plants.notes: Acquired from AES/EGCO for $1.9B in March 2018. 28 years old.

plants.name: Masinloc Unit 3
plants.notes: New build expansion. Only 5 years old.

plants.name: SMC Mariveles Units 1-4
plants.notes: Newest plant - only 1 year old. Commissioned despite 2020 moratorium (exempted).

b1_calculation.notes: 47.6% at-risk based on 40-year technical life. Older plants (Sual, Masinloc 1-2) retire by 2039. Newer plants (Masinloc 3, Limay, Malita, Mariveles) retire 2057-2060. Still FAILS <20% threshold.

coal_plants.source: SMC Annual Report FY2024; Global Energy Monitor

emissions.disclosure_status: Limited - power segment not separately disclosed

scope1.estimation_methodology: Estimated from generation mix: Coal (16,897 GWh × 0.95 tCO2/MWh) + Gas (2,512 GWh × 0.45 tCO2/MWh)
scope1.notes: SMC Group total ~9 MtCO2e; power segment estimated at 61% = ~5.5 MtCO2e (appears underreported). Using generation-based estimate.

intensity_trend.notes: Cannot determine - no historical emissions disclosure

emissions_targets.2030_target: 30% reduction in GHG intensity vs 2021 baseline
emissions_targets.2040_target: 70% reduction in GHG intensity vs 2021 baseline

emissions.cdp_climate_notes: CDP questionnaire completed; score not publicly available

sustainability_reporting.notes: GHG Protocol methodology used; not 'in accordance' with GRI

perpetual_securities.notes: PHP 234 billion - if treated as debt, total leverage significantly higher

credit_rating.notes: Local rating only. DELIBERATELY UNRATED by international agencies to avoid negative perception and restrictive covenants.

historical_context.notes: Balance sheet from 2023; cash flow from 2022. 2024 showed significant improvement but structural leverage concerns remain.

net_zero.notes: 2050 target is 10 years LATE vs SBTi Power Sector standard (2040). Implementation details sparse per IEEFA.

sbti.notes: NO SBTi commitment. SBTi Power Sector standard requires net-zero by 2040 with coal phase-out 2030-2040.

coal_policy.no_new_coal_notes: ACTIVELY EXPANDING coal capacity. 700 MW under construction (Masinloc 4-5). 60% of Philippines coal pipeline.
coal_policy.coal_exit_notes: NO announced coal exit timeline. No retirement dates for ANY plants.
coal_policy.source: SMC Annual Report; Global Energy Monitor

re_targets.notes: 10 GW RE target by 2028 (announced 2018) - only 0.5% progress. SIGNIFICANTLY BEHIND.

capex_allocation.notes: 76% of 2022 bond proceeds allocated to fossil fuels vs 0.1% to solar. Clean CAPEX below 50% threshold.

emissions_targets.notes: Intensity targets allow absolute emissions to increase with growth

green_finance.notes: 2024 perpetual securities exclude coal financing but not classified as green bond

bess_project.notes: Most significant clean energy achievement. Largest BESS network in Philippines.

debt_to_ebitda.notes: HIGH leverage at 5.3x. If perpetual securities (PHP 234B) treated as debt, ratio rises to ~9.5x.

ffo_to_debt_pct.notes: NEGATIVE - 2022 OCF was negative. May have improved in 2024.

debt_to_capital_pct.notes: Below threshold at 48%. However, if perpetual securities included as debt, rises to 62%.

credit_metrics_b5.notes: Only 2 of 5 metrics pass (Interest Coverage, Debt/Capital). Debt/EBITDA at 5.3x is elevated. FFO/Debt is NEGATIVE due to 2022 cash burn. Current Ratio exactly at 1.0x threshold. B5 FAILS. Credit profile is weak - deliberately unrated by international agencies.
credit_metrics_b5.source: SMCGP SEC Forms 17-A; SMC Annual Report

carbon_pricing_exposure.current_price_note: Philippines has NO carbon tax or ETS. HB 11375 passed House but not enacted.

b2_calculation.notes: At $0 carbon price, CCE = 0%. B2 PASSES despite high coal exposure.

sensitivity.notes: SMC would fail B2 at carbon price >$12/tCO2

carbon_pricing_exposure.notes: No carbon price means B2 automatically passes despite being largest coal developer in Philippines.

sp_global.notes: Profile exists but company does not participate

data_gaps: Power segment emissions not separately disclosed from SMC Group

b1_stranded_asset_risk.rationale: 47.6% of coal capacity (1,788 MW) retires after 2040. Newer plants (Masinloc 3, Limay, Malita, Mariveles) commissioned 2017-2024 with 30+ years remaining life. NO announced retirement dates.
b1_stranded_asset_risk.data_quality: medium - no company retirement dates; using 40-year technical life

b2_carbon_cost_exposure.rationale: CCE = (17M tCO2 × $0/tCO2) / $991M EBITDA = 0%. Philippines has no carbon pricing.

b3_lcoe_crossover.rationale: At $0 carbon price, coal SRMC ($60/MWh) cheaper than solar+storage ($94/MWh). No thermal displaced.

b4_transition_alignment.rationale: Clean CAPEX 23.3% < 50% threshold. No SBTi commitment. Net-zero 2050 (10 years late). Actively expanding coal. 0.5% progress on RE targets.

b5_balance_sheet_stress.rationale: Only 2/5 metrics pass. Debt/EBITDA 5.3x (FAIL), FFO/Debt negative (FAIL), Current Ratio 1.0x (FAIL). Deliberately unrated by international agencies.

benchmark_assessment.notes: SMC is the TRANSITION LAGGARD in Philippines. Scores 2/5 (Low-medium tier). B2/B3 pass only due to absence of carbon pricing. Fails B1 (47.6% coal at-risk), B4 (23% clean CAPEX, no SBTi, actively expanding coal), B5 (weak credit metrics). DWS divested April 2023 due to ESG concerns. Most aggressive coal developer - 60% of Philippines coal pipeline.

data_sources.source: SMC Annual Report and Investor Briefing FY2024
Global Energy Monitor Coal Plant Tracker
IEEFA Report: San Miguel Global Power (September 2023)

analyst_notes: SMC Global Power is the largest power producer in Philippines and the most aggressive coal developer - accounting for 60% of the country's coal pipeline. Key findings: (1) 3,756 MW operating coal + 700 MW under construction; (2) Zero operational solar/wind despite 10 GW RE target announced in 2018 (0.5% progress); (3) 76% of 2022 bond proceeds went to fossil fuels vs 0.1% to solar; (4) NO SBTi commitment; 2050 net-zero is 10 years late vs sector standard; (5) Deliberately unrated by international agencies; (6) DWS divested April 2023 citing ESG concerns. POSITIVE: 1 GW BESS network completed (largest in Philippines). BOTTOM LINE: SMC is a transition laggard with significant greenwashing risk - stated ambitions do not match investment reality.
